raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding American Expresss improper denial of a credit card dispute involving goods not as described ( fraudulent fulfillment ). 

I purchased XXXX XXXX  from a merchant XXXX in the amount of {$410.00} including tax on XX/XX/2026. Instead I received XXXX bottles of nail polishitems that are completely unrelated to what I ordered. 

I attempted to resolve the issue directly with the merchant starting on XX/XX/2026, but they failed to provide a refund. I then filed a dispute with American Express. 

American Express denied my first claim when the merchant stated I never contacted them. American Express allowed me to open a second claim after providing proof of my contact with XXXX. 

American Express denied my second claim based on the merchants return policy and timing requirements. This is inappropriate, as this is not a return dispute. It is a failure to deliver the purchased goods, and return timelines should not apply. Additionally, XXXX did not ask to return anything to them. 

American Express appears to have misclassified the dispute and relied on procedural defenses rather than evaluating the actual merchandise delivered. 

I have provided documentation, including order confirmation and photos of the incorrect items received. 

I request that American Express reclassify this dispute as goods not as described, conduct a proper review, and issue a full refund.
